title: In solution digestion for protein standards (Urea)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4nnav","text":"Digestion of protein standards, such as albumin, is a common practice to keep in-house quality standards for technical calibration of Mass-Spectrometry instruments. Here we describe a protocol to digest three different protein standards and their preparation for injection in a Mass-Spectrometry instrument.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"dgl59","text":"Make the Urea and ABC buffers fresh on the same morning of the experiment. steps: Weight 1 mg of each standard and resuspend in 100 µL of 8 M Urea in ABC.
Add DTT to a final concentration of 25 mM, incubate for 30 min at Room temperature in a shaker (1200 rpm)
Add IAA to a final concentration of 55 mM, incubate for 30 min Room temperature in the dark .
Add 5 µL of Lys-C (stock at 1 µg/µL ), for 3-4 hours at 37 °C (This is adding 1 µg of Lys-C per 20 µg of protein).

Dilute sample 5X times (add 400 µL) with ABC buffer This step is performed because trypsin does not work in high urea concentrations. Be careful to use the ABC buffer and NOT the one with Urea and ABC when diluting.
Add 5 µL of trypsin (stock at 1 µg/µL , resuspend in 20 µL of 0.1 % TFA) to each sample. Incubate O/N at 37 °C (This is adding 1 µg of trypsin per 20 µg of protein).
Equilibrate the Sep-Pak C-18 column:Flush the column with 5 ml of solution B or Acetonitrile
Add 10 ml of solution A. Add the digested sample into the 10 ml of solution A and let it into the Sep-Pak column slowly.
Wash the sample with 10 ml of Buffer A
Elute with 2 x 600 µl of solution B (collect your purified sample in a 2 mL tube)
Dry down purified peptides completely in a speed-vac. Resuspend in Buffer A (up to 20 µM)
creator: Cristina CARDENAL PERALTA
